title: Legality-State Expert With Actual Pirelli Compound Mapping
summary: FIA rules require two different dry specifications unless wets/inters were used, and official race previews show that weekend `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` map to different underlying C-compounds by race.
rationale: FIA rules require two different dry specifications unless wets/inters were used, and official race previews show that weekend `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` map to different underlying C-compounds by race. Current features treat `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` generically and only partially encode dry-compound usage, missing rule pressure and true softness context.
implementation_hint: Join official race-weekend tyre nominations by `Year,Race` so each row knows whether weekend hard/medium/soft were e.g. `C2/C3/C4` or `C3/C4/C5`. Build a small dry-race expert with features like `dry_specs_used_so_far`, `mandatory_spec_used`, `wet_exception_active`, `second_dry_stop_pressure`, and `weekend_softness_index`.
expected_effect: Improves late-race and one-stop/two-stop boundary decisions where legality or actual compound hardness matters more than generic tyre age.
risk: Requires robust external joins and special-casing of non-standard events such as pre-season testing; wrong mapping coverage could hurt more than help.
